_comment: Transparent priority weights. score = sum(weight_i * signal_i) + learned_adjustments. Edit freely; the feedback loop nudges these in adjustments.json, never here.

triage._comment: Thresholds for the cheap rule-based classifier before any Claude call.

models._comment: Which claude -p model handles cheap triage vs richer summaries. Uses your Claude Code subscription, no API key.

_diff_comment: The Diff view compares a session's worktree branch against this base (committed + uncommitted). PR tasks use the real gh pr diff instead. Falls back to origin/HEAD, then main/master.
_soul_source_comment: Canonical SOUL.md lives in the CRM (Jarvis voice-learner distills it nightly from real emails + calls). The nightly dream pulls it read-only via `gh api repos/<repo>/contents/<path>` into config/SOUL.md. Best-effort: if gh is missing/unauthed/offline, the last synced copy is kept. Requires gh authed to the (private) CRM repo on the server.
_pane_widths_comment: Default A|B split fraction (Pane A share) per task mode. PR/review tasks (Pane B = Diff) want a wider diff, so Overview is narrower. Operator drags override per-mode and persist.
_pr_repos_comment: GitHub repos to scan for open PRs (gh CLI). Surfaced as REVIEW_DIFF items you can diff + merge from the UI. A PR whose branch is already owned by a working session is NOT double-carded — that session is tagged + floored instead. Add your own as ["owner/repo"]; empty = PR scanning off.
_pr_scan_interval_ms_comment: How often to poll GitHub for open PRs (ms). 300000 = every 5 min.
_pr_min_priority_comment: Floor on the ORGANIC score of any PR-backed item (standalone pr-card or a working session tagged with an open PR), so an open PR is plainly visible in the queue by default. Operator gestures apply AFTER the floor: snoozing or an h/l lower CAN take a PR below it (the snooze decays back to the floored score), and a manual set-score is absolute.
_kanban_comment: OPTIONAL kanban integration: when the ready Claude-work queue has fewer than min_active_tasks items, top it up with the highest-priority kanban cards (kind='kanban') from this folder of .md cards. Columns scanned in the order below. Leave "" to disable the kanban feature entirely.
_kanban_auto_comment: kanban_auto_launch is the master switch (then `npm run restart`). When ON and the ready queue is below min_active_tasks, the engine launches the single top surfaced card — EVERY card auto-launches regardless of #-ready / NEEDS-INFO / H classification (2026-06-11, operator decision): the launch prompt tells the Claude session it was auto-started, passes along any open triage questions, and instructs it to research first and ask the operator in-session for anything it can't resolve. Still at most ONE launch per tick AND per 30-min cooldown window. The cooldown is load-bearing anti-stampede: readyClaudeCount() lags (a freshly launched session isn't a ready 'claude' item for seconds-to-minutes), so without it the queue still reads low and it would stampede. A failed launch consumes the cooldown too (2026-06-10 incident).
_kanban_repo_comment: Repo whose kanban cards/worktrees ClaudeOS manages, and the default repo for new sessions. Set to YOUR repo path, e.g. "/home/you/code/your-repo". Leave "" if you only ever pass an explicit repo path to `cli.js launch`.
_sessions_repos_comment: Repos offered in the ← (new session) launcher. Defaults to [kanban_repo] if set. Add your own, e.g. ["/home/you/code/your-repo"].
_polls_comment: tick_interval_ms = main engine pipeline cadence (server); terminal_poll_ms = live-terminal capture refresh (renderer).
_auto_open_comment: When a COMPLEX_DECISION card is first focused, auto-open the live terminal so the operator drops into the session (quick-answer options still shown). Set false to disable.
_snooze_comment: Snooze applies this (negative) score penalty so the item sinks toward the bottom but STAYS VISIBLE (you can still see/do it later). The penalty then DECAYS LINEARLY back to 0 over snooze_recover_hours, so the item slowly climbs back to its natural rank (at -100 over 5h it recovers 20 points/hour). Stacks per re-snooze (from the current decayed value), capped at 3x. Cleared when the task is completed/decided.
_reap_completed_tmux_comment: Nightly (~03:00) the orphan tmux of any session COMPLETED via Ctrl+G e more than this many hours ago is killed, so dozens of done tasks don't leave dozens of idle terminals/Claude processes running. Only completed (Ctrl+G e) sessions are touched — never dismissed/snoozed/active ones — and the git worktree + db row are kept (undo still works within the window). Set 0 to disable.
_auto_complete_idle_comment: Nightly (~03:00) any claude session whose transcript hasn't changed for this many hours is AUTO-COMPLETED: archived out of the sessions panel, its pending queue items superseded, and both its terminals reaped — teammate workers, one-shot background jobs and abandoned sessions that nobody ever Ctrl+G e's (also covers dead sessions: they stop writing). Pinned sessions, the session being viewed, shells, and sessions with a still-future ETA are never touched, and NO kanban card is moved (an idle-dead session does not mean its task is done). Set 0 to disable.
_auto_diff_comment: PR and REVIEW_DIFF tasks default to the full GitHub-style split diff inline (right pane) on first focus — no need to press d. Set false to disable.
_auto_html_comment: When the task you're viewing has an HTML visualization (one a session wrote, or in the /visualize folder), auto-surface it in the LEFT pane (Pane A) on first focus and whenever a NEW html appears — no need to press h. Never steals keyboard focus from the terminal. Set false to disable.
_eta_comment: Long-running silent sessions: PASSIVE time-left estimation — NOTHING is ever typed into a session (the old '/eta' keystroke probe is gone; it polluted live sessions). For a long-running session (actively WORKING, or parked/UNKNOWN and quiet for probe_after_min minutes) the engine reads the session's OWN recent output (tmux pane capture, else transcript tail) and lets Haiku estimate the time left; a printed 'eta: 50m' | 'eta: done' | 'eta: none' marker is also parsed directly for free. A session with a future ETA is HELD OUT of the Task Queue (blue '~Xm left' in the roster) until expiry; on expiry a fresh estimate is fetched (bypassing reprobe_min down to a 60s floor), and if none lands the session surfaces in the Task Queue — a backgrounded task ALWAYS resurfaces eventually (only the operator removes it, via Ctrl+G E). reprobe_min throttles estimates while waiting. probe_after_min = production 60 (1h); lower it (e.g. 4) only for live testing. Set enabled:false to disable.
_state_gate_comment: The READY GATE (card 288): the hard guarantee that nothing enters Up Next while it's still outputting tokens or merely waiting on its own script. Layered: (0) cheap heuristics; (1) a FREE double-sample — the transcript signature (mtime+size+tail-hash) AND the process-tree CPU must be byte-stable/quiet across double_sample_gap_ms before a session is even eligible (any motion => still streaming, stay hidden); (2) a model final classifier (claude -p, state_gate.model — haiku for cost (0 false-surfaces on the verifier goldset, npm run eval:verifier; sonnet scores 72/72 exact but ~3.3x the rate-limit weight — flip back if DONE-vs-needs-input precision matters more than cost)) reads the tail and picks WORKING | WAITING_ON_SELF (blocked on its own job/babysitting -> hidden) | WAITING_ON_OPERATOR (surface) | DONE (surface). EVERY alive candidate is classified before it may surface — there is NO recency window anymore (long-quiet 'kicked off the script, will check back' sessions used to leak past the model after 2 min of silence). The verdict is cached by transcript mtime (one call per transcript state) and re-checked at most once per min_verify_interval_ms; settle_ms avoids classifying a session that wrote in the last beat; cpu_busy_frac (0..1) is the CPU fraction that counts as 'still computing'. Self-bounds: fail_open_attempts = after that many consecutive failed/timed-out classifier checks the stable heuristic state surfaces anyway (a down model can never strand a ready session); self_wait_ttl_min = a WAITING_ON_SELF verdict only hides while the session has been quiet less than this (0=forever) — past it the session surfaces as low-prio idle so a silently-finished job is never lost (the ETA probe can re-hold it with a concrete time-left). classifier_enabled:false falls back to heuristics + double-sample only. COST: min_verify_interval_ms=30000 caps re-verification of actively-writing sessions at ~2/min/session (was 6s); a session's FIRST verdict is never throttled (due = !cached), so fresh questions still surface within ~settle+gap seconds — only re-checks of changed transcripts wait.
_learning_comment: How strongly each feedback signal teaches the nightly dream (multiplies learning_rate, default 0.05). Just PICKING a non-top task or snoozing is a SILENT signal — VERY low signal, teaches almost nothing (implicit_learn_weight). A bare h/l up/down nudge teaches 5x (direction_learn_weight). Typing a REASON ('I don't want this because X') teaches 15x (reason_learn_weight) — the dominant signal. Effective: silent 0.1×lr · bare h/l 5×lr · h/l+reason 15×lr (a reason teaches 150x a silent pick, 3x a bare nudge).
_auto_continue_comment: Auto-continue a session STALLED after a tool call (the 'type continue and it wakes up' failure). VERY conservative (operator 2026-06-15): fires ONLY when the last transcript entry is an unanswered tool_result whose tool-caller meant to keep going (stop_reason tool_use), the detector itself reads WORKING (never a question/done/idle), there is no pending interactive prompt, the CPU tree is idle, AND nothing has been written for quiet_ms = 600000 (10 MINUTES). 10 min of total silence in a stuck-tool-call shape = a real stall, not thinking/generating (those write incrementally; a network-bound generation never goes 10 min with zero writes). Then sends `message` to the pane, up to max_attempts per stall, retry_ms apart; a transcript advance (it worked) resets it; after max_attempts it SURFACES as needs-you. Every nudge is logged to .run/auto-continue.jsonl. Excludes pending questions, done turns, in-flight tools, teammates, babysit-flagged panes. enabled:false to disable.
